title: hypericum hircinum subsp. majus
intro: Hypericum hircinum subsp. majus, also known as St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant native to Europe, western Asia, and north Africa, characterized by its yellow flowers and reddish-colored leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antidepressant effects. The health benefits of Hypericum hircinum subsp. majus include its ability to reduce anxiety and depression symptoms, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules, although its use can interact with certain medications and should be consulted with a healthcare professional.
